en: I went out with my mother today.

ab: viviva’ane tabelengane ka mathao, va’iyane ka ikakece si ikaapa’a, besebese ka mabalesebese.
ch: 白天的天空很晴朗，天氣不冷也不熱，風很涼爽。
en: The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

ab: ’iyakay kiname dida’ane taswaoswaovane kwane na ming-pao, kokoli’i ka wapava’i bwatiname, sawkoa malabeabee.
ch: 我們在草地上吃著麵包，陽光灑在身上，非常溫暖。
en: We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

ab: viva’anenga ka tahelengane ka wabaythi matileange.
en: In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

ab: kyaboboo kake titinane, akwani tabelengane patasini ka ’atadileleangini?
ch: 我問媽媽，為甚麼天空是紅色的。
en: I asked my mother why the sky is red.

ab: titina ka ’apobaka, kadromane ka no sebee ami.
ch: 媽媽說，可能是颱風要來了。
en: Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

ab: ma’awbo tabelengane ka kadroa na koongo, ’abwale na taiyaw.
ch: 晚上天上沒有雲，星星出來了。
en: There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

ch: 在回家的路上，我跟上天許願。
en: On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

ab: saamanga si i’awkay na sesbee, ’api ’abebebee la kake.
ch: 希望颱風不要來，我還想再出去玩。
en: I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.
